poem.content:
The alley whispers a gospel of dust,
Zero hour nears, a judgment we must
Approve. The board sits, faces grim and still,
While wind, a hungry ghost, begins to drill.

Protect the soup, a broth of fading light,
Against the coming dark, the endless night.
Each soul a vivid chunk, of memory's taste,
Dispersed and lost, a terrible, swift waste.

The wise ones speak of amounts due, and paid,
A grim accounting, choices we have made.
My position's clear, though fear begins to act,
A chilling tremor, a truth I can’t retract.

The topic hangs, suspended in the air,
As consequences bloom beyond compare.
A dance of shadows, a final, fleeting grace,
Before the fall from this appointed place.

And if it ends, and all is washed away,
May hope, at least, illuminate the day.
May truth remain, though veiled so very deep,
A promise whispered while the world's asleep.
